steps.title: Map the passage structure
steps.content: The text first describes Marquez's yarn-on-photograph method, then gives two examples: one honoring Prince and another honoring an ordinary young girl. It ends by connecting both approaches to her goal of showing power and interconnectedness.

steps.title: Reject structures with added topics
steps.content:
• Choice A adds cultural background and future projects, neither of which appears.
  • Choice B adds other artists and collaboration, which the passage does not discuss.
  • Choice C adds yarn's history and the popularity of Marquez's work, both unsupported.
The answer is D.
